rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly provided whitelist
Preserve existing AADL properties and connections when inserting AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Use appropriate scope for assumptions and guarantees
Preserve existing model elements during fusion
Validate inserted annex blocks for type consistency
Consider component type vs implementation scope when placing annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax
Use `assume` and `guarantee` keywords for environmental contracts
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `const` for constant definitions
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not mix component type and implementation scope annex blocks
Avoid unresolved references to AADL properties inside AGREE annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement beyond component type and implementation scopes
